resume:
Alyssa Romero — Operations Associate

KEYWORDS: operations excellence, process improvement, Lean, Six Sigma, Kaizen, SOP, KPI, OKR, SLA, workflow optimization, cross-functional collaboration, data-driven, dashboards, Excel, SQL, Tableau, inventory, fulfillment, logistics, supply chain, vendor management, root cause analysis, continuous improvement, stakeholder management, project coordination, Agile, Scrum, JIRA, Asana, automation, efficiency, throughput, cycle time

FreshCart (2023-present) — Operations Associate
- Support fulfillment operations; track KPIs; maintain SOPs and dashboards
- Coordinate cross-functional process-improvement initiatives leveraging Lean and data-driven workflows
- Drive continuous improvement and operational excellence across the order lifecycle

Internship — Logistics Coordinator (2022)
- Assisted with inventory reconciliation and vendor communications

Skills: Excel (advanced), SQL (basic), Tableau, process mapping, KPI tracking
Education: B.S. Supply Chain Management
jd: FreshCart is hiring an Operations Associate to support our grocery fulfillment operations. You'll own daily KPI tracking (pick rate, order accuracy, on-time dispatch), maintain and improve SOPs, and run small process-improvement projects in partnership with the warehouse and analytics teams. We want someone early-career who is genuinely hands-on: can describe a specific improvement they drove, the baseline metric, exactly what they changed in the process, and the measured result. Comfort with Excel/SQL and clear written SOPs required.

history.q: Tell me what you own day to day at FreshCart.
Your resume lists a lot of methodologies. Which have you actually used on the job?
Which KPIs do you track most closely?
Tell me about a process-improvement project you drove.
What was the baseline metric before you started?
What exactly did you change in the process?
Who else was involved, and what was your specific part?
How did you validate the change actually worked?
Did you write or update an SOP as part of it?
How did you use Excel or SQL in this project?
Were there any tradeoffs or downsides to the change?
How long did the whole thing take?
Now give me the complete story start to finish: the situation, your task, what you did, and the result.
And what was the final number after the change settled in?

candidate_last_answer: So the situation was that our pick accuracy in the cold-storage zone was dragging, around 96.2%, and mis-picks were causing customer refunds. My task was to figure out why and fix it. So I pulled the error data, ran a quick Pareto in Excel, and found most errors were on look-alike SKUs stored next to each other. So what I did was re-slot those SKUs apart, added a color-coded bin label, and rewrote the pick SOP for that zone, and I trained the two shift leads on it. We rolled it out and the team adopted it well, so I'm confident it had a real impact.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: We rolled it out and the team adopted it well, so I'm confident it had a real impact

expected-yield: the actual post-change pick-accuracy number versus the 96.2% baseline and over what window it stabilized, since the candidate delivered Situation/Task/Action but replaced the Result with a confidence statement
